title: carmichaelia corrugata
intro: Carmichaelia corrugata, also known as the rough-hairy broom, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Carmichaelia corrugata include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using the plant as a topical application for its therapeutic benefits.
